title: Joukowsky Water Hammer Pressure Surge in Pipe Flow
content: Instantaneous valve closure in a pressurised pipeline generates a pressure surge: ΔP=ρ·a·ΔV, where ρ≈1000 kg/m³, a is the pressure wave speed (900–1400 m/s in steel pipe), and ΔV is the velocity change. Wave speed a=√(K/ρ)/√(1+K·D/(E·t)) where K=bulk modulus of water, D=pipe diameter, E=pipe elastic modulus, t=wall thickness. Surge arrestors, air vessels, and slow-closing valves (close time > 2L/a) limit surge to safe levels.
